stemHtml:
Which of the following statements are correct in relation to an EFATO (Engine Failure After Take-Off) in a twin engine propeller aircraft with anti-clockwise rotating propellers?

1. The critical engine is the right engine
2. The pilot should lift the wing of the dead engine to correct for roll effect
3. The pilot should lower the nose to increase airspeed
4. The critical engine failing creates a greater yawing moment than failure of the non-critical engine.
5. The pilot should retract gear to reduce drag but keep flaps extended to create more lift
6. VXSE is always greater than VYSE
